title: ACDP Lifecycle Event (0.3.0)
description: A signed, append-only event recorded in registry_state.lifecycle_events (RFC-ACDP-0013, acdp/0.3.0 — promoting the RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.1 reservation). Additive minor, so this schema lives in the v0.1.0 namespace (VERSIONING.md). Where a signature is present, the event preimage is the JCS canonicalization of this object minus 'signature'; the actor signs the ASCII bytes of 'sha256:<hex(SHA-256(preimage))>' exactly as RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.8 / RFC-ACDP-0010 §5 (RFC-ACDP-0013 §5).

event_id.description: UUID (RFC 9562 canonical lowercase form) minted by the actor. Unique within the context's lifecycle_events array (RFC-ACDP-0013 §4).

ctx_id.description: The affected context. MUST equal the ctx_id of the context whose registry_state carries this event — binding a signed event to exactly one context (RFC-ACDP-0013 §4).

event_type.description: Event kind. Open vocabulary registered in registries/lifecycle-event-types.md; v1 defines 'retracted' and 'republished'. Unknown values matching the pattern MUST be tolerated with no status effect (RFC-ACDP-0013 §7.3).

occurred_at.description: When the actor performed the action: canonical millisecond-precision RFC 3339 UTC (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.3). Ordering of events is by array position, never by this field (RFC-ACDP-0013 §4.1, §7.1).

actor.description: The DID performing the action: body.agent_id for producer-initiated events; the registry's DID for registry-initiated events (RFC-ACDP-0013 §4, §6).

reason.description: Optional human-readable explanation. Informational only; MUST NOT drive automated decisions (RFC-ACDP-0013 §4).

signature.description: Signature over the event hash (RFC-ACDP-0013 §5). REQUIRED on producer-initiated events (key resolved per RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.11); SHOULD be present on registry-initiated events (registries advertising acdp-registry-receipts MUST sign, SHOULD use the RFC-ACDP-0010 receipt signing key).

$comment: Closed schema, as acdp-registry-receipt.schema.json / acdp-lineage-head-receipt.schema.json (RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.3.1 openness map): every member is signed where a signature is present, so an unknown member would change the preimage. Envelope extensions require a future version; the event_type VOCABULARY is open, the event OBJECT is closed.
